title: Combinational Circuits: Multiplexer and Decoder
summary: Covers the multiplexer (MUX) and decoder as fundamental combinational building blocks, including their implementation and use for Boole function realisation.
content_points: Multiplexer (2^n data inputs, n select inputs, 1 output): routes selected data input to output.
Implement any n-variable function: truth table outputs → data inputs; variables → select inputs.
Folding: use n-1 variables as select inputs; assign the remaining variable (or constant) to data inputs – halves the MUX size.
Decoder (n inputs, 2^n outputs): activates exactly one output equal to the binary input value.
Each decoder output corresponds to one minterm; enables efficient sum-of-minterms implementation.
learning_objectives: Describe the operation of a multiplexer and decoder
Implement a Boole function using a MUX
Apply folding to reduce MUX size
